replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: anything is fine as long as you made it - a bought panel or a bought battery isn't allowed and charging a battery from an external bought power supply also isn't allowed I am afraid - if you use a battery you must have charged it from a machine you made to charge batteries - so if you charge some home made batteries from a homemade water wheel that's fine but charging batteries from a ready made battery charger isn't - I hope that is clear - cheers

author: @mrdewilliams
text:
My work schedule will not allow me much time to do this for the competition.  I am up to my eyeballs doing data security audits through the end of November. But if someone is looking for an idea to try...

Using the principles from "Weird Wind Generator No Blades, No Moving Parts" (Jun 7, 2020) video, attach the magnet or the coil to a cable or fishing line tensioned to a pole.  Arrange 4-5 of these around the pole, and place it out in the wind.  You should have a low-wind power generating aeolian harp.  Stan  Hershonik (@UC9dflkiYLJ-ps8YuSB7fhNQ channel) has several simple aeolian harp how-to videos that you should be able to adapt to use Robert's "No Blades" generator.  The more "strings", the more power you can potentially generate.

Functional, power generating art.

I was planning to build an aeolian harp for the back patio after the new year, but after seeing this video, the idea of adding the power generation to it just jumped to mind.  I would love to see what others make of it.  We'll see what I can make after the new year comes.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I am sorry you won't get the time mate but in awe of you sharing the idea - that was a real kindness - cheers and I hope you do get to it in the new year and work slackens offf for you - cheers

Mr Smith, is this competition open to say new tech & if the tech happens to be a "wow" factor that would have the audience be running for the  "nah it must be fake" button immediately, because some things are beyond some "googelogists" perception of the universe & how it actually works & yet from experience some scientists too, sadly enough.
So can there be a possible funding/marketing prize as the ultimate goal, to commercialize or is there another platform to use?!  It is obvious that some tech really wakes up the wrong crowd, if put on the air, it can be risky considering the times we live in with power hungry people waiting for inventors to make a dumb move...talking out of experience not wanting to make the 2nd dumb move!!

author: @antwerpduk8426
text:
Could you use a series of reluctance generators on a wheel axel to recharge the batteries on an electric car? You could also have wind reluctance generators on the air intakes, and fans within the wheels. 

Is it possible?

I had an idea a few hours ago, if you have a gas, or filament stove cooker, you could have a metal tubing filled with water placed over the flames, or welded to the filament, the water would heat and turn into steam. You have a small valve, and a small hole for the steam to then exit (once pressurised), the pressure from the steam turns the reluctance generator that cups the steam (like tiny bowls, or spoons instead of a fan. You could also use this design in stoves, fireplaces, or anywhere heat is produced.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I think so mate and I like the idea here - get building !! lol
